definition: Convert peak lists to normalized sparse matrices or GPU batches, use m/z tolerance-aware sparse matrix products or GPU kernels to compute candidate spectral cosine scores, and optionally rerank top hits with precise greedy or modified cosine.

scale_profile.best_for: Repository-scale MS/MS library searches and molecular-networking audits.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Tens of billions of comparisons with GPU or sparse-matrix batching in specialized implementations.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Low-resolution spectra require wide tolerance windows that make binned approximations diverge from exact peak matching.

required_inputs: peak lists with mz and intensity

tunable_parameters.name: rerank_precise_cosine
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls whether fast candidates are rescored with exact peak matching.

quality_gates: Compare accelerated scores to a reference cosine implementation on a subset.
Report tolerance, bin width, intensity scaling, and normalization.
Rerank top hits with precise cosine when binning could affect conclusions.
failure_modes: Wide m/z tolerance can inflate binned cosine scores.
Precursor-shift handling changes the biological or chemical interpretation.
Dense output matrices are infeasible; threshold or top-k storage is required.
example_usage: python sparse_peak_cosine_topk.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --peak-col peaks --bin-width 0.001 --top-k 50

citations.title: BLINK enables ultrafast tandem mass spectrometry cosine similarity scoring
citations.evidence: Uses sparse matrix operations for tandem mass-spectrometry cosine scoring and reports large speedups over loop-based scoring.

citations.title: SimMS: a GPU-accelerated cosine similarity implementation for tandem mass spectrometry
citations.evidence: Implements GPU-accelerated greedy and modified cosine scoring for MS/MS spectra with large speedups and high agreement with MatchMS.
